Set by\\n// handleRestart right before sessions.shutdown(); cleared after exit anyway.\\nlet restartPendingChatId: number | undefined;\\n\\n// Best-effort: pipe fetch response body into a file.\\nasync function downloadToFile(url: string, destPath: string): Promise<void> {\\n  const res = await fetch(url);\\n  if (!res.ok || !res.body) {\\n    throw new Error(`download failed: ${res.status} ${res.statusText}`);\\n  }\\n  await mkdir(path.dirname(destPath), { recursive: true });\\n  // Node 18+: Readable.fromWeb for WHATWG stream → Node stream.\\n  const { Readable } = await import(\\\"node:stream\\\");\\n  const nodeStream = Readable.fromWeb(res.body as any);\\n  await pipeline(nodeStream, createWriteStream(destPath));\\n}\\n\\nfunction sanitizeName(name: string): string {\\n  return name.replace(/[\\\\\\\\/:*?\\\"<>|\\\\x00-\\\\x1f]/g, \\\"_\\\").slice(0, 120) || \\\"file\\\";\\n}\\n\\nconst DOC_CONVERTIBLE_EXTS = new Set([\\\".pdf\\\", \\\".docx\\\", \\\".pptx\\\", \\\".xlsx\\\", \\\".xls\\\", \\\".html\\\", \\\".htm\\\"]);\\n\\n\\n// ─── Pending /schedule wizard state ────────────────────────────────────────\\nconst PENDING_SCHEDULE_TTL_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;\\n\\n// ─── 群組模式：防止 bot 互相無限乒乓 ─────────────────────────────────────\\nconst GROUP_MAX_BOT_TURNS = Number(process.env.GROUP_MAX_BOT_TURNS ?? \\\"5\\\");\\nconst BOT_ALLOWED_UPDATES = [\\\"message\\\", \\\"callback_query\\\", \\\"message_reaction\\\"] as const;\\nconst groupTurnCounters = new Map<string, number>();\\n\\n// Relay idle-loop prevention: managed by commands/relay.ts (getRelayLoopGuard)\\n\\n// ─── AB-7b goal continuation timer map ────────────────────────────────────\\n// Per-chat setTimeout handle; set when a goal continuation is scheduled,\\n// cleared (a) when the continuation fires or (b) when a real user message\\n// arrives before it fires (user preempt). The delay gives the user a short\\n// window to type something that should be handled before the agent auto-\\n// continues.\\n\\n// How often (ms) we're allowed to update the streaming reply message.\\nconst STREAM_EDIT_INTERVAL_MS = config.streamEditIntervalMs;\\n\\nfunction isAllowed(ctx: Context): boolean {\\n  if (config.allowedUserIds.length === 0) return true;\\n  const uid = ctx.from?.id;\\n  return typeof uid === \\\"number\\\" && config.allowedUserIds.includes(uid);\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Command menu (inline + reply keyboards)\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// A single source of truth for the user-facing command list. Used to render\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Command handlers (reusable)\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Each handleX(ctx) contains the full body for one command, so it can be\\n// invoked both from bot.command() (slash command) and from the inline-button\\n// callback_query dispatcher below. help:<name>   → show that command's description (or return to menu)\\n//   run:<name>    → execute the command (dispatched through COMMAND_HANDLERS)\\nbot.on(\\\"callback_query:data\\\", async (ctx) => {\\n  if (!isAllowed(ctx)) {\\n    await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"未授權。\\\" });\\n    return;\\n  }\\n  const data = ctx.callbackQuery.data;\\n  const [kind, name] = data.split(\\\":\\\", ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"未知的指令。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    await ctx.editMessageText(renderCommandDetail(spec), {\\n      parse_mode: \\\"HTML\\\",\\n      reply_markup: ${chatId}] MoA plan execution failed:`, err);\\n        bot.api.sendMessage(chatId, `❌ MoA 計畫執行失敗：${(err as Error).message.slice(0, 300)}`).catch(() => {});\\n      });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"未知的動作。\\\" });\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"DT\\\") {\\n    // Dream todo：報告按鈕點擊 → 把 STATE.md High Priority 丟給主 session\\n    await handleDreamTodoCallback(ctx);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"sess\\\") {\\n    // /session 多 session 管理：sess:sw:<id>（切換）| sess:new（新對話）\\n    await handleSessionCallback(ctx, data);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"A\\\") {\\n    // ASK callback: data is `A:{question_id}:{key}`. split(\\\":\\\", 2) above\\n    // only gave us the first two fields, so re-split here to get the key.\\n    const parts = data.split(\\\":\\\");\\n    const questionId = parts[1] || \\\"\\\";\\n    const key = parts.slice(2).join(\\\":\\\"); // tolerate accidental ':' in key (shouldn't happen given ASK_KEY_RE)\\n    if (!questionId || !key) {\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"無效的選擇。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const msgId = ctx.callbackQuery.message?.message_id;\\n    const entries = msgId !== undefined ? askRegistry.get(msgId) : undefined;\\n    const entry = entries?.find((e) => e.questionId === questionId);\\n    const opt = entry?.options.find((o) => o.key === key);\\n    const label = opt?.label ?? key; // fallback to key if registry lost (e.g. after restart)\\n\\n    // Strip the inline keyboard so the same buttons can't be re-tapped, but do\\n    // NOT rewrite the message content. Session is looked up the same\\n    // way the message handler does — ctx.chat is present on callback_query.\\n    const chatId = ctx.chat?.id;\\n    if (chatId === undefined) return;\\n\\n    // ─── /schedule wizard hooks ───────────────────────────────────────────\\n    // The schedule wizard uses ASK but does NOT want the choice forwarded to\\n    // the agent: selections drive bridge-local state transitions instead.\\n    if (questionId === \\\"sched_kind\\\") {\\n      await handleSchedKindChoice(ctx, chatId, key);\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (questionId.startsWith(\\\"sched_t_\\\")) {\\n      await handleSchedTimeChoice(ctx, chatId, questionId, key);\\n      return;\\n    }\\n\\n    let session: ChatSession;\\n    try {\\n      session = await sessions.get(chatId, ctx.from?.id);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      await ctx.reply(`Failed to start Kiro agent: ${(err as Error).message}`);\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    // Record the choice in the follow-up reply itself (header), so the original\\n    // button message stays intact AND no extra message is needed.\\n    await runPrompt(ctx, session, `[ASK:${questionId}] ${key}`, {\\n      kind: \\\"user\\\",\\n      replyHeader: `我選擇了：${label}`,\\n    });\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"specialist\\\") {\\n    if (!name || !isLocalSpecialist(name)) {\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"分身不存在。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const chatId = ctx.chat!.id;\\n    startProxy(chatId, name);\\n    await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: `進入 ${name}` });\\n    await ctx.editMessageText(`🤖 已進入 *${name}* proxy 模式。\\\\n打 /back 退出。`, { parse_mode: \\\"Markdown\\\" });\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"sched\\\") {\\n    // Schedule management buttons. Tell them, then try to redraw the list so the\\n      // phantom row disappears.\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"排程已不存在。\\\" });\\n      const chatId = ctx.chat?.id;\\n      if (chatId !== undefined) {\\n        const { text, keyboard } = renderScheduleList(chatId);\\n        try {\\n          await ctx.editMessageText(text, {\\n            reply_markup: keyboard ?? { inline_keyboard: [] },\\n          });\\n        } catch (err) {\\n          // editMessageText throws \\\"message is not modified\\\" if nothing\\n          // changed — that's fine, nothing to do.\\n          console.warn(\\n            `[sched] redraw after stale rm failed:`,\\n            (err as Error).message,\\n          );\\n        }\\n      }\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (ctx.from?.id !== undefined && entry.userId !== ctx.from.id) {\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"這不是你的排程。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const chatId = ctx.chat?.id;\\n    if (chatId !== undefined && entry.chatId !== chatId) {\\n      // Button from another chat somehow reached here — refuse.\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"排程不屬於這個聊天室。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    scheduler.disarm(id);\\n    await scheduleStore.remove(id);\\n    await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: `已移除 ${id}。` });\\n\\n    // Redraw the list in place so the user sees the new state without\\n    // scrolling back to re-run /schedule list.\\n    if (chatId !== undefined) {\\n      const { text, keyboard } = renderScheduleList(chatId);\\n      try {\\n        await ctx.editMessageText(text, {\\n          reply_markup: keyboard ?? <observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Imported backfillFactEmbeddings for integration into bridge startup</title>
  <subtitle>Added import statement to enable calling embedding backfill during initialization</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>index.ts line 34 modified to import backfillFactEmbeddings from memory-db.js module</fact>
    <fact>Import added alongside existing initMemoryDb and syncFactsToDb imports</fact>
    <fact>Enables calling the new backfill function during bridge startup sequence</fact>
    <fact>Part of two-part fix: eager cache population + retroactive backfill</fact>

  <narrative>Import statement updated to expose the newly created backfillFactEmbeddings function at the bridge initialization layer. This is the second step in resolving the embedding cache gap - after adding eager caching to insertFact and creating the backfill function in memory-db.ts, the function must now be integrated into the bridge startup sequence in index.ts. The import addition allows the main initialization flow (around line 1415-1430) to call backfill after syncFactsToDb completes, progressively populating embeddings for the existing 195 facts in the database that currently have zero matching cache entries.</narrative>

    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>

content.thinking:
This is a critical code change. The user is integrating the backfillFactEmbeddings function into the bridge's startup sequence. Let me analyze what's happening:

1. After syncFactsToDb completes (which syncs facts from master log to database)
2. The code now calls backfillFactEmbeddings() in a fire-and-forget manner (void)
3. The backfill runs asynchronously without blocking bridge startup
4. Success: logs the number of embeddings backfilled if > 0
5. Failure: logs a warning but doesn't crash the startup

This is the final piece of the embedding cache fix. The architecture is:
- **Eager caching**: insertFact now calls getEmbeddingCached in fire-and-forget mode for new facts
- **Retroactive repair**: backfillFactEmbeddings runs at startup to populate cache for existing facts
- **Non-blocking**: Uses void to fire-and-forget so it doesn't delay bridge startup
- **Progressive**: Idempotent design means each restart progressively fills the gap

The comment is in Chinese explaining that vectorSearch depends on embedding_cache having fact vectors, which is exactly the root cause we identified earlier.

This completes the bugfix for the zero fact recall issue in the hit-log.

  <narrative>Completed the three-part fix for zero fact recalls by integrating backfillFactEmbeddings into bridge startup. The function executes immediately after syncFactsToDb completes, progressively repairing the embedding cache gap for the existing 195 facts. The fire-and-forget pattern ensures the async embedding computation doesn't block bot.start() - the bridge comes online immediately while backfill runs in background. On first restart after this fix deploys, the backfill will discover ~195 facts missing embeddings and populate the cache via repeated getEmbeddingCached calls. Subsequent restarts will find fewer/zero missing entries as the gap closes. The idempotent LEFT JOIN design means this runs safely on every startup without duplicate work. Combined with eager caching in insertFact, this establishes continuous embedding coverage: new facts get cached immediately, existing facts get repaired at startup, ensuring vectorSearch's JOIN can finally find matching facts and restore semantic fact recall functionality.</narrative>

content.thinking:
The user is editing the SKILLLINT_PROMPT to add additional handling for orphan entries. Specifically, they're adding a rule to skip orphan entries that have notes indicating they're not actually skills (like custom commands or false positives).

The edit adds:
"entry 的 notes 欄位若已標明「非 skill / custom command / false positive」則跳過不報（已知誤報，如 uk-conventions 是 command 不是 skill）。"

This translates to: "If the entry's notes field already indicates 'not a skill / custom command / false positive' then skip and don't report (known false positive, e.g., uk-conventions is a command not a skill)."

This is a refinement to the skilllint command's prompt to handle the uk-conventions case that was identified in STATE.md as a High Priority issue. Rather than removing uk-conventions from skill-usage.json (which is one of the pending tasks), they're updating the prompt to tell the agent to skip reporting entries that have notes documenting they're custom commands.

This is interesting - it's a softer approach than direct removal. The entry stays in the registry but won't be reported as an orphan in future skilllint runs.
